title: Ensure route table changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure route table changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Routing tables are used to route network traffic between subnets and to network gateways. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to route tables.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to route tables will help ensure that all VPC traffic flows through the expected path and prevent any accidental or intentional modifications that may lead to uncontrolled network traffic. An alarm should be triggered every time an AWS API call is performed to create, replace, delete, or disassociate a route table.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{($.eventSource = ec2.amazonaws.com) && ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DisassociateRouteTable) }" 6. Note the <route-table-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <route-table-changes-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<route-table-changes-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for route table changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateRoute) || ($.eventName = CreateRouteTable) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRoute) || ($.eventName = ReplaceRouteTableAssociation) || ($.eventName = DeleteRouteTable) || ($.eventName = DeleteRoute) || ($.eventName = DisassociateRouteTable) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <route-table-changes-alarm> --metric-name <route-table-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs route table events (e.g., CreateRoute, DeleteRouteTable), but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist. These changes are captured but not actively monitored unless configured.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls or Boto3 Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to list and describe CloudTrail trails.
     - Method: `describe_trails()`
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to get the trail status.
     - Method: `get_trail_status(Name=<trail-name>)`
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to get the event selectors for the trail.
     - Method: `get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail-name>)`
   - Use `boto3.client('logs')` to describe metric filters for CloudWatch log groups.
     - Method: `describe_metric_filters(LogGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)`
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` to describe alarms.
     - Method: `describe_alarms()`
   - Use `boto3.client('sns')` to list subscriptions for an SNS topic.
     - Method: `list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)`

2. **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable)**:
   - Listing CloudTrail trails: `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
   - Getting trail status: `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
   - Getting event selectors: `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
   - Describing metric filters: `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
   - Describing CloudWatch alarms: `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms`
   - Listing SNS subscriptions: `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - List all CloudTrail trails in the account.
   - Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails.
   - Gather the associated CloudWatch log group name from the CloudTrail configuration.
   - Check for active metric filters and alarms associated with the log group.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail exists with IsLogging set to TRUE.
   - Ensure the trail captures management events (IncludeManagementEvents = true and ReadWriteType = All).
   - Verify the presence of a metric filter with the correct filter pattern for route table changes.
   - Ensure at least one alarm exists that uses the created metric filter.
   - Confirm that the SNS topic associated with the alarm has at least one subscriber.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - Pass: Active multi-region CloudTrail with correct logging status, appropriate event selectors, metric filter with matching filter pattern, an associated alarm, and at least one SNS subscriber.
   - Fail: Any of the above configuration is missing or incorrect. 

This structured approach leverages AWS APIs to fully automate the verification process for monitoring changes to route tables, resulting in an efficient compliance audit.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of AWS APIs, boto3, and CLI commands allows for the automated verification of route table change monitoring.
- The assessment effectively utilizes AWS SDK capabilities and CLI commands to automate the compliance check.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid and appropriate for the task.
- However, the program name `aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_monitoring` could be more descriptive. It does not specify that it checks for metric filters and alarms, which are crucial parts of the control.
- The description of the steps is comprehensive, but it could benefit from explicitly mentioning the filter pattern used to detect route table changes.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Program Name:** Consider renaming to `aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_metric_filter_alarm_monitoring` to better reflect the scope of the checks being performed.
- **Filter Pattern:** The assessment should specify the exact filter pattern used to detect route table changes. For example, the pattern might look for specific API calls like `CreateRoute`, `DeleteRoute`, `ReplaceRoute`, etc.
- **AWS Config:** Consider mentioning AWS Config as an additional service that could be used to monitor changes to route tables. AWS Config can provide a historical view of configuration changes.
- **Event Selectors:** Ensure that the event selectors capture both management and data events if applicable, as this could affect the detection of certain API calls.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and uses appropriate AWS services and methods to automate the compliance check. The suggested improvements are minor and do not significantly impact the overall validity of the approach.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to be more descriptive and include the specific filter pattern for route table changes. Consider mentioning AWS Config as an additional monitoring tool.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the compliance control for monitoring route table changes can be automated using AWS services such as CloudTrail and CloudWatch. The necessary AWS APIs and CLI commands are available to set up and verify metric filters and alarms for route table changes, making automation feasible and efficient. The use of boto3 and AWS CLI commands allows for comprehensive monitoring and alerting, aligning with the control's requirements.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_route_table_changes_metric_filter_alarm_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(LogGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)`
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - CloudTrail trail configuration for multi-region and log group association
  - Metric filters for specific API calls (`CreateRoute`, `DeleteRoute`, `ReplaceRoute`)
  - CloudWatch alarms linked to the metric filters
  - SNS topic subscriptions for alarm notifications
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - At least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the correct log group
  - Metric filters configured for route table change events
  - CloudWatch alarms set up and linked to the metric filters
  - SNS topic with active subscriptions for alarm notifications
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - Absence of any of the above configurations or incorrect settings

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions; both concurred on the automated approach, with the second opinion suggesting a more descriptive program name, which has been adopted.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v6.0.0
